preamble: The transformation from a nasalized mid vowel to a diphthong involves the development of a semi-vowel off-glide, often as part of a merger in a language's phonological history, particularly in the Romance family.

examples.note: Historical development where Latin endings like -onem, -anum, and -anem merged into the nasal diphthong -ão (mapped here as 'au' for the diphthongal quality).

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
